channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Giant octopuses may have ruled the oceans 100 million years ago - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Giant octopuses may have ruled the oceans 100 million years ago  Phys.org
  2. Fossil discovery reveals ‘hidden’ apex predator that rivaled marine reptiles 100 million years ago  CNN
  3. Scientists just discovered a 60-foot-long, kraken-like octopus  The Washington Post
  4. Jaw fossils suggest a 60-foot octopus was the ‘kraken’ of the Cretaceous  National Geographic
  5. ‘Kraken-like’ giant octopuses 100m years ago crunched bones of prey  The Guardian

item.title: Interstellar invader comet 3I/ATLAS formed in a world much colder than the solar system - Space
item.description:
1. Interstellar invader comet 3I/ATLAS formed in a world much colder than the solar system  Space

  4. Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS Left a Trail of Methane in its Wake  Universe Today
  5. Water D/H in 3I/ATLAS as a probe of formation conditions in another planetary system  Nature

item.title: 24,000-year-old zombie worm gets thawed, immediately starts reproducing - Yahoo
item.description:
1. 24,000-year-old zombie worm gets thawed, immediately starts reproducing  Yahoo
  2. A worm frozen for an impressive 46,000 years in Siberia returns to life in a laboratory, reproduces after the Neanderthal era, and reveals an unbelievable scientific phenomenon that could change everything we know about survival on Earth and beyond.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  3. Scientists Revived a 24,000-Year-Old Animal Frozen in Siberia, It Woke Up and Started Reproducing  Indian Defence Review
  4. A Rotifer Got Buried In The Siberian Permafrost. 24,000 Years Later, It Came Back To Life.  IFLScience
  5. Meet the Ice Age survivor: The rotifer that cheated death for 24,000 years and started reproducing after coming back to life  The Times of India

item.title: NASA’s DART Spacecraft Changed An Asteroid’s Orbit Around The Sun - DOGO News
item.description:
1. NASA’s DART Spacecraft Changed An Asteroid’s Orbit Around The Sun  DOGO News
  2. For the first time, humanity significantly altered the orbit of a celestial body  ECOticias.com
  3. In 2022, NASA shot a spacecraft at an asteroid at 6.6 km per second and changed its orbit by 33 minutes — now Europe's Hera probe is traveling there to inspect the damage and make planetary defense an exact science.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  4. NASA makes history by shifting an asteroid’s path around the sun  MSN

item.title: First Ever Complete Skeletal Cast of 30-Foot Extinct Alligator–See it in Georgia - Good News Network
item.description:
1. First Ever Complete Skeletal Cast of 30-Foot Extinct Alligator–See it in Georgia  Good News Network
  2. Scientists have reconstructed for the first time the complete skeleton of the largest crocodile that ever existed — it was almost 10 meters long, weighed as much as an elephant, and was capable of dragging dinosaurs into the water.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  3. This 31-foot “terror croc” ate dinosaurs. Now it’s back  Science Daily
